title: security.sbom.generated.v1
description: Wire format for the `security.sbom.generated.v1` event published by sbom-pipeline (:4007) and consumed by security-service (:4003) and GitOps automation (.github/workflows/security.yml). LOCKED 2026-06-12 (O-3.6). Projection boundary: security-service :4003 (NOT the Python agents). One event per SBOM artifact. O-3.7 additions: `sbom_fingerprint_algorithm` and `sbom_fingerprint_format` fields (SecurityArchitect redline; aligns with in-toto / SLSA v1.0 attestation convention). Schema grows from 11 to 13 required fields.

schema.description: Event discriminator. MUST be the literal string `security.sbom.generated.v1`.

sbom_id.description: Stable identifier for the SBOM artifact. Format: `sbom-<YYYY-MM-DD>-<git-short-sha>-<scope>` (scope ∈ {monorepo, service, package, container, fs, git-tree}). Used as the basename in `security/sboms/<sbom_id>.<format>` and as the `id` field in `security/sboms/index.json`.

sbom_fingerprint.description: Cryptographic fingerprint of the SBOM artifact bytes, used as the audit-log correlation key in SecurityArchitect's S2.8 §3.6 mitigations. Format: `sha256:<hex>` (or `sha512:<hex>` / `blake3:<hex>` — see `sbom_fingerprint_algorithm`). The digest is computed over the bytes described by `sbom_fingerprint_format` (default: RFC 8785 / JCS canonicalized CycloneDX JSON). Consumers MUST recompute using the algorithm + format and reject on mismatch. The 'sha256:' prefix encodes the algorithm; the canonicalization is encoded separately in `sbom_fingerprint_format`. Convention aligns with sigstore / in-toto / SLSA v1.0 attestation patterns.

sbom_fingerprint_algorithm.description: Hash algorithm used to compute `sbom_fingerprint`. The algorithm prefix in `sbom_fingerprint` MUST match this field. Default for O-3.7: 'sha256'. Open-ended for future migration to 'sha512' or 'blake3'. The algorithm alone is not sufficient to recompute the fingerprint — the input bytes (defined by `sbom_fingerprint_format`) must also be reproduced. O-3.7 addition (SecurityArchitect redline; aligns with in-toto / SLSA v1.0 attestation convention of explicitly describing the digest algorithm).

sbom_fingerprint_format.description: Versioned string describing the canonicalization applied to the SBOM bytes before hashing. Versioned contract: 'cyclonedx-json+canonicalized-jcs' (default — RFC 8785 / JCS canonicalized CycloneDX JSON), 'cyclonedx-json+raw' (no canonicalization — legacy interop only, will be deprecated), 'spdx-json+canonicalized-jcs' (future — when SPDX is the primary format), 'spdx-json+raw' (future). The format string is a contract, not an implementation detail. Consumers MUST reproduce the exact same input bytes (subject to the format) before hashing. Two semantically-identical SBOMs hashed with JCS vs. without JCS will produce different fingerprints — encoding the canonicalization eliminates an entire class of 'why doesn't my hash match?' debugging. O-3.7 addition (SecurityArchitect redline; future-proofs the wire contract for Sprint 3+ format additions).

sbom_format.description: Primary serialization format of the SBOM on disk (`security/sboms/<sbom_id>.<sbom_format>`). Producers publish the primary format only; secondary format is optional and not referenced in the event.

sbom_path.description: Relative repo path to the SBOM file inside `security/sboms/`. Producers MUST write to `<repo-root>/security/sboms/<sbom_id>.<sbom_format>` (folder contract LOCKED in security/README.md § 'Folder contracts').

provenance_path.description: Relative repo path to the SLSA v1.0 in-toto provenance attestation. Optional but RECOMMENDED for `container` and `monorepo` scopes. Sibling of the SBOM: `security/sboms/<sbom_id>/provenance.intoto.jsonl`.

scope.description: Subject classification. Aligns with SRE `repo_shape` label (LOCKED 2026-06-12, D2 verdict).

subject.description: Opaque reference to the scanned subject. Conventional prefixes: `repo:owner/name`, `image:registry/name:tag`, `path:relative/path`, `fs:absolute/path`. Producers SHOULD use `owner/name` for `monorepo` scope.

subject_fingerprint.description: Cryptographic identifier of the subject. For `monorepo` and `git-tree` scope: full git SHA. For `container` scope: image digest (`sha256:<hex>`). For `service` / `package` scope: same as the parent repo SHA. Used to correlate SBOM events to vuln scan targets and to deduplicate retry runs.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the SBOM bytes were finalized and written to disk. Producers MUST use UTC with millisecond precision and a `Z` suffix.

generator.description: Tool identifier and version that produced the SBOM. Convention: `<tool>:<semver>`. Examples: `syft:1.18.0`, `cdxgen:10.10.2`. Used to filter vulnerabilities by scanner coverage and to attribute drift in `security/vulns/weekly-*.md`.

components_count.description: Total number of components (packages) enumerated in the SBOM. Used by SRE's `sbom_size_bucket` label projection (LOCKED 2026-06-12, D1 verdict: small<100, medium<1k, large<10k, xlarge<50k, xxlarge≥50k).

tool_invocation.description: Optional sub-record capturing the tool invocation parameters needed for SLSA v1.0 source verification (LOCKED with the SBOM pipeline, see `security/README.md` § 'SBOM pipeline contract'). Recorded in the provenance attestation; the event references it by path. Not strictly required in the event payload but recommended for `container` and `monorepo` scopes.

labels.description: Optional free-form labels for downstream filtering. Conventional keys: `team`, `tier`, `compliance-scope`. MUST NOT include PII.
